finding.summary: Clean Guitar Child has healthy dynamics (crest factor 12 dB, LRA 8 LU) and safe headroom (true peak -5.5 dBFS, sample peak -6.0 dBFS, integrated LUFS -20). The 1/3-octave spectrum shows a clear downward tilt: 100 Hz at -18 dB is 6 dB above 1 kHz (-24 dB) and 12 dB above 4 kHz (-30 dB). For a clean electric guitar routed to a bus, this indicates a bass-heavy/low-mid-weighted balance with relatively weak presence-region energy, which can read as dark or muddy. Silence fraction is low (5%), so level statistics are reliable and not diluted. The FX chain is empty, so no corrective EQ or dynamics is currently applied on this track; parent bus ('Guitar Bus') may be doing some processing, but that is outside the scope of this single-track diagnosis.
finding.probable_cause: Without an EQ on the track, the natural pickup/cabinet and recording tone is unprocessed. The 100 Hz region being notably stronger than 1 kHz and 4 kHz suggests either a guitar with bass-heavy voicing, a mic position favoring low body resonance, or simply the absence of a high-pass/low-shelf cut that most clean guitar parts benefit from. The empty FX chain means no corrective or character EQ is in place to rebalance the tilt.
finding.confidence_reason: Only three 1/3-octave bands were provided (100 Hz, 1 kHz, 4 kHz). This is too sparse to pinpoint a specific problematic frequency, distinguish a true low-end buildup from a natural guitar timbre, or determine whether 1 kHz or 4 kHz needs boosting versus 100 Hz needing cutting. No stereo image data is available (stereo is null), so mid/side balance cannot be considered. Any specific EQ frequency, gain, or Q would be a guess.

evidence_refs.description: Three 1/3-octave bands showing a -12 dB tilt from 100 Hz to 4 kHz.
12 dB crest factor indicates healthy, uncompressed dynamics.
-20 LUFS integrated loudness with RMS -18 dB and true peak -5.5 dBFS confirms safe level and headroom.
0.05 silence fraction — statistics are not diluted by dead air.
Empty FX chain — no EQ or dynamics processing on the track itself.

proposal.reason: The track's level, headroom, and dynamics are all in a healthy range and do not require adjustment. The observed spectral tilt suggests an EQ move could help, but with only three 1/3-octave bands of spectrum data and an empty FX chain, I cannot identify a specific frequency, gain, or Q value to set, nor any existing FX parameter to bypass as a preview. Naming a specific EQ band (e.g., 'cut 80–120 Hz') without finer-grained spectrum data would be a guess, not a diagnosis. The honest result is no safe, evidence-backed move on this single track at this time; a re-capture with finer spectrum resolution or bus-level analysis would be needed to act on the tonal observation.
proposal.goal: Re-capture with higher-resolution spectrum or verify tonal tilt via bus analysis.
proposal.rejection_reason: Only 3 1/3-octave bands and no FX params make any set_fx_param / set_fx_bypass unverifiable.
